title: Delicate Jordanian Beef Mansaf
description: A peppery, flaky beef cutlet served over fermented kimchi and rice, cooked on a raclette grill for a quick weeknight Middle Eastern dinner, Ayurvedic tamasic friendly.
instructions: Rinse rice and cook in 2 cups water until fluffy, then set aside.
Season beef cutlets with salt, pepper, cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, and lemon juice; let rest 10 minutes.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a raclette grill pan over medium heat.
Sear each beef cutlet on the grill until a crust forms, about 3-4 minutes per side, then remove to a plate.
In the same pan, add chopped kimchi and sauté for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Return the beef cutlets to the pan, spoon the kimchi over them, and grill for an additional minute to combine flavors.
Plate a bed of cooked rice, top with the grilled beef and kimchi mixture.
Garnish with a swirl of yogurt and a sprinkle of fresh herbs (optional) and serve immediately.
